title: Net SGST Reimbursement
summary: New industries receive 100% net SGST reimbursement for 10 years, capped at 150% of Fixed Capital Investment (FCI). Instalments vary by enterprise size.
keyBenefits: Micro: single instalment; Small: 3 annual; Medium: 5 annual
eligibility: New manufacturing or service industries commencing production on or after 1 Nov 2024
applyAt: Dept. of Commerce & Industry / Single Window Portal

title: EPF Contribution Reimbursement
summary: 75% of EPF contribution reimbursed for skilled and semi-skilled CG domicile employees for up to 5 years from the date of commercial production. Capped at ₹1 crore per year.
keyBenefits: Duration: up to 5 years from commercial production
eligibility: New industries; employees must be CG domicile skilled/semi-skilled workers with wages under ₹50,000/month

title: Marginal Money Subsidy — SC/ST, Women & Special Categories
summary: 25% capital investment subsidy for new micro and small enterprises set up by SC/ST, women, ex-servicemen, Agniveers, Divyangjan, third-gender, and surrendered Naxalites. Maximum subsidy ₹1 crore.
keyBenefits: Covers micro and small enterprises only
eligibility: New micro/small enterprises by SC/ST, Women, Ex-servicemen, Agniveers, Divyangjan, Third-gender, Surrendered Naxalites
applyAt: Dept. of Commerce & Industry / District Industries Centre

title: Mandi Tax Exemption — Agro & Food Processing
summary: 100% exemption from mandi tax for new large enterprises in agriculture, food processing, and biofuel/ethanol sectors for 5 years from first raw material purchase.
keyBenefits: Total cap: 75% of Fixed Capital Investment
Raw material must be sourced from state mandis or direct farmers
eligibility: New large enterprises in agro, food processing, biofuel/ethanol sectors (eligible industries only)

title: Training Expense Reimbursement for New Employees
summary: One-time reimbursement of training expenses equal to one month's wage (or ₹15,000 per person, whichever is less) for new CG domicile employees earning below ₹50,000/month.
keyBenefits: Reimbursement: 1 month wage or ₹15,000, whichever is less
Applicable for 5 years from commercial production
Capped at 100% of Fixed Capital Investment
First claim payable 1 year after employee appointment
eligibility: New industries; employees must be CG domicile, skilled or semi-skilled, wages below ₹50,000/month

title: Land Premium Exemption in Industrial Areas
summary: 100% exemption on land premium for land allotted in industrial areas managed by the Directorate of Industries/CSIDC. Lease rent fixed at ₹1 per acre per year.
keyBenefits: Lease rent: ₹1 per acre per year
Applicable for land in CSIDC-managed industrial areas
Maintenance, water, street light charges payable separately
eligibility: New industries allotted land in CSIDC / Directorate of Industries industrial areas

title: Transport Cost Reimbursement for MSMEs
summary: 50% reimbursement of transport costs (excluding mining products) for eligible industries. Annual cap of ₹50 lakh per year for up to 5 years.
eligibility: Eligible industries under IDP 2024–30 (non-mining)

title: Divyangjan & Surrendered Naxalite Employment Subsidy
summary: 40% salary reimbursement for 5 years for industries employing Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, and surrendered Naxalites from CG. Annual cap of ₹5 lakh per year.
keyBenefits: Covers Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, surrendered Naxalites
eligibility: Industries offering permanent employment to Divyangjan, retired Agniveers, surrendered Naxalites from CG

title: Startup Seed Funding — Proof of Concept Stage
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Proof of Concept (PoC) stage startups can receive seed funding up to ₹10 lakh for developing a Minimum Viable Product (MVP), under the CG Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30.
keyBenefits: Seed funding up to ₹10 lakh per startup
eligibility: Startups recognised by Government of Chhattisgarh under Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30

title: Startup Capital Fund — ₹100 Crore Corpus
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: ₹100 crore CG Startup Capital Fund to invest in recognised startups via SEBI-registered AIFs (50:50 co-investment model). Separate ₹50 crore Credit Risk Fund for collateral-free loans up to ₹1 crore.
keyBenefits: ₹100 crore state corpus for startup investments
₹50 crore Credit Risk Fund — collateral-free loans up to ₹1 crore
eligibility: State government-recognised startups; AIF investment subject to fund manager criteria

title: Startup Interest Subsidy on Term Loans
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: 100% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹50 lakh at a maximum interest rate of 10% for 5 years. Additionally, up to 75% interest subsidy under IDP 2024–30 for term loans and working capital.
keyBenefits: 100% interest subsidy on loans up to ₹50 lakh
Max interest rate covered: 10% per annum
eligibility: State-recognised startups under CG Innovation & Startup Promotion Policy 2025–30

title: Startup Workspace Rental Reimbursement
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: 60% assistance on monthly workspace rent/lease for 3 years from date of state startup recognition. Capped at ₹15,000 per month. Applicable for incubation centres and co-working spaces too.
keyBenefits: Covers co-working spaces and incubation centres
eligibility: State-recognised startups with leased/rented workspaces or incubation centre seats

title: Startup Digital Advertising Reimbursement
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Up to ₹3 lakh per startup reimbursed for digital advertising expenses on platforms including Meta (Facebook/Instagram), YouTube Ads, LinkedIn, Google Ads, and Microsoft Ads.
keyBenefits: Reimbursement up to ₹3 lakh per startup
Eligible platforms: Meta, YouTube, LinkedIn, Google Ads, Microsoft Ads
eligibility: State-recognised startups; receipts and invoices must be in startup's name

title: Women Startup Seat Reservation & Incentive Bonus
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Startups with women founders and special category founders receive 10% reserved seats in state-supported incubators plus an additional 10% on all applicable subsidies and incentives.
keyBenefits: 10% incubator seat reservation for women/special category founders
Additional 10% bonus on all applicable incentives
eligibility: Startups with women founders or special category founders under 2025–30 policy

title: Startup Employment Subsidy — CG Domicile Workers
department: Dept. of Commerce & Industry — Startup Cell
summary: Monthly salary subsidy for startups employing 10+ on-roll CG domicile workers: ₹6,000/month per female employee and ₹5,000/month per male employee for 5 years.
keyBenefits: Duration: 5 years from start of operations
eligibility: State-recognised startups with 10+ on-roll CG domicile employees

title: Krishak Unnati Yojana — Paddy Procurement Bonus
summary: Farmers receive ₹3,100 per quintal for paddy procurement — covering the difference between MSP and the promised rate. Budget allocation: ₹10,000 crore in 2025. Biggest agriculture scheme in the state.
keyBenefits: Covers difference between central MSP and state-promised rate
eligibility: Paddy farmers registered in Chhattisgarh
applyAt: Primary Agriculture Cooperative Societies (PACS) / Mandi offices

title: Godhan Nyay Yojana — Cow Dung Purchase Scheme
summary: Government purchases cow dung from cattle owners at ₹2 per kg through Gauthans (cattle shelters). Dung is converted into vermicompost and organic manure by women's SHGs. Gaudham Yojana launched in 2026 to extend this framework.
keyBenefits: Cow dung purchased at ₹2 per kg
Payments every 15 days to registered beneficiaries
Women SHGs earn income from vermicompost production
Gaudham Yojana (2026): 29 new Surabhi Gaudhams inaugurated
eligibility: CG domicile cattle owners registered at local Gauthan/Ward Office
applyAt: Nearest Gauthan, SLRM Centre, or Ward Office

title: Free Electricity for Agriculture Pumps
summary: Free electricity provided to farmers for irrigation pumps up to 5 HP. Budget allocation: ₹3,500 crore in 2025–26. Aimed at reducing cost of farming and increasing irrigation coverage.
keyBenefits: Free electricity for pumps up to 5 HP
eligibility: Farmers with agriculture pump connections up to 5 HP in CG

title: Deendayal Upadhyay Bhumihin Krishi Majdoor Yojana
summary: ₹10,000 annual financial assistance for landless agricultural labourers in Chhattisgarh. Targets over 5.62 lakh landless farm workers.
keyBenefits: Direct benefit transfer to bank accounts
eligibility: Landless agricultural labourers who are CG domicile residents
policyRef: Deendayal Upadhyay Bhumihin Krishi Majdoor Yojana

title: Mahtari Vandana Yojana — Women Monthly Financial Support
summary: ₹1,000 per month (₹12,000 per year) direct financial assistance to eligible married women of Chhattisgarh. Budget 2025–26 allocation: ₹5,500 crore. Payments made monthly to bank accounts.
eligibility: Married women who are CG domicile residents; income and other eligibility criteria apply

title: Mukhyamantri Saksham Suraksha Yojana — Women Self-Employment Loan
summary: Women can access loans up to ₹1 lakh at 5% interest rate to start small businesses. Aimed at economically empowering women in the unorganised sector.
eligibility: Women who are CG residents and part of Mahtari Vandana Yojana or qualifying SHGs
applyAt: Women & Child Development Dept. / District offices

title: Mukhyamantri Nirman Shramik Pension Sahayata Yojana
summary: ₹1,500 per month pension for registered construction workers aged 60 years and above. Part of the Chhattisgarh Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
keyBenefits: Administered by CG Building & Construction Workers Welfare Board
Apply: Mukhyamantri Shramik Siyan Sahayata Portal
eligibility: Registered construction workers aged 60 years and above
applyAt: CG Labour Department / District labour office
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Mukhyamantri Nirman Shramik Mrityu Evam Divyang Sahayata
summary: Financial aid to construction workers and their families in case of accidental death (₹2 lakh compensation) or disability (₹1 lakh compensation). Requires registration with the Board.
keyBenefits: Accidental death compensation: ₹2 lakh to family
eligibility: Construction workers registered with the CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Mukhyamantri Shramik Siyan Sahayata Yojana
summary: One-time financial assistance of ₹20,000 for registered construction workers aged 60 years and above. Complements the pension scheme with a one-time lump sum.
keyBenefits: Apply online via Labour Department portal
eligibility: Registered construction workers aged 60+ who have completed minimum contribution period
applyAt: CG Labour Department portal / District labour office

title: Sister Maternity Assistance Scheme (Prastuti Sahayata)
summary: Maternity cash assistance to women construction workers and wives of male construction workers registered with the Board. Provides financial support during pregnancy and childbirth.
keyBenefits: Cash maternity benefit for registered women construction workers
Also covers wives of registered male construction workers
Administered by CG Construction Workers Welfare Board
Multiple maternity schemes cover cleaning, contract, domestic workers too
eligibility: Women registered with CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, or wives of registered male workers
policyRef: CG Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Board

title: Chhattisgarh Udyam Kranti Yojana — Youth Self-Employment Loan
summary: Interest-free or subsidised loans with 50% subsidy to encourage self-employment for youth. Helps young people start businesses and become self-reliant.
eligibility: Youth of Chhattisgarh intending to start businesses; domicile certificate required
applyAt: District Industries Centre / Commerce & Industry dept.

title: Tendupatta (Kendu Leaf) Collection — MSP Support
department: Dept. of Forest / Minor Forest Produce
summary: Government purchases Tendu (Kendu) leaves from tribal and forest collectors at a minimum support price of ₹5,500 per standard bag (मानक बोरा), increased from ₹4,000.
eligibility: Tribal and forest-dwelling communities in CG collecting Tendu leaves
applyAt: Forest Department / Minor Forest Produce cooperative

title: Bijli Bill Half Yojana — 50% Electricity Subsidy
summary: 50% subsidy on electricity bills for consumption up to 400 units per month for over 40 lakh consumers. Single-bulb consumers get free electricity up to 30 units.
keyBenefits: 50% subsidy for consumption up to 400 units/month
Free electricity up to 30 units for single-bulb households
Applied automatically — no separate application needed
eligibility: Domestic electricity consumers in Chhattisgarh consuming up to 400 units/month

title: Mukhyamantri Ucch Shiksha Rin Byaj Anudan Yojana — Education Loan Subsidy
summary: Interest-free education loans up to ₹4 lakh for CG students pursuing higher education. Covers interest component on eligible education loans.
keyBenefits: For higher education in recognised institutions
Apply online through CG Higher Education portal
eligibility: CG domicile students enrolled in recognised higher education institutions
policyRef: Mukhyamantri Ucch Shiksha Rin Byaj Anudan Yojana

title: Tourism Homestay Policy — Bastar & Sarguja
summary: New homestay policy for promotion of tourism specifically in Bastar and Sarguja divisions. Incentives for private and community investment in eco-ethnic tourism infrastructure. Chhattisgarh Tourism Board (CTB) established in 2002 to oversee.
keyBenefits: Dedicated homestay incentive policy for Bastar & Sarguja
Budget allocation in 2025–26 for tourism development
Reduced minimum investment threshold for hotels and resorts in Bastar & Sarguja
CTB project approval: up to 2 applications per district annually via DLTC
eligibility: Individuals and communities in Bastar and Sarguja divisions setting up homestays or eco-tourism units
applyAt: District Level Tourism Committee (DLTC) → Chhattisgarh Tourism Board (CTB)

title: Quality Certification Subsidy for MSMEs
summary: New and existing MSMEs in the general category receive 50% reimbursement of expenses incurred in obtaining quality certifications (e.g. ISO, BIS, FSSAI, etc.).
eligibility: New or existing micro, small, and medium enterprises in CG in the general category
applyAt: Dept. of Commerce & Industry / District Industries Centre

title: Incubator Establishment Grant
summary: Incubators established during the industrial policy tenure receive 40% of establishment expenses (up to ₹40 lakh). Annual operational grants: ₹5 lakh/year at divisional headquarters; ₹3 lakh/year elsewhere.
keyBenefits: Annual ops grant at divisional HQ: ₹5 lakh/year for 5 years
Annual ops grant at other districts: ₹3 lakh/year for 5 years
Encourages technical institutions to set up incubators
eligibility: Incubators established in technical institutions during IDP 2024–30 tenure
